result: **Goal: Add a Time Crystal mode** — a discrete time crystal simulator where cells spontaneously break time-translation symmetry, forming stable periodic oscillations (period-doubling, subharmonic responses) that emerge from local interaction rules rather than external driving. Cells would have spin states subject to periodic Floquet driving with disorder, exhibiting the hallmark DTC behavior: the system responds at a fraction of the drive frequency, robust against perturbations. This complements the recently added Quantum Game of Life and Spacetime Fabric modes, pushing further into cutting-edge condensed matter physics (Wilczek 2012, experimental realization 2016–2017).
